title: cytisus capitatus subsp. supinus
intro: Cytisus capitatus subsp. supinus, also known as the "Hairy Kidney Vetch," is a perennial plant species in the legume family, native to the Mediterranean region. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and diuretic effects. The plant is rich in alkaloids, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds that have been shown to have various health benefits, including reducing blood pressure, improving kidney function, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in infusions, decoctions, and tinctures to support urinary health and alleviate symptoms associated with kidney stones and urinary tract infections.
